citations.summary: The provided text is an excerpt from the case of Lumley v Gye, which established that an action lies for maliciously procuring a breach of contract for exclusive personal services, even if the employment has not yet commenced, provided damage results.
citations.holding: The provided text is an excerpt from the case of Lumley v Gye, which established that an action lies for maliciously procuring a breach of contract for exclusive personal services, even if the employment has not yet commenced, provided damage results. It also contains a dissenting opinion arguing that such actions are strictly limited to master-servant relationships as defined by the Statute of Labourers.
citations.howUsed: Cited in support of the argument.
citations.reasoning: The brief appears to cite this case out of context — the source does not address the proposition it is cited for.
citations.recommendation: Reformulate to match the actual holding.
